crime.value: Murder of Francesco Cenci, likely by hired assassins Marzio and Olympio
crime.quote: Marzio and Olympio had been seen prowling round the castle, and that the Neapolitan police had received orders

victim.value: Francesco Cenci, whose character and relationship with his family made him a target
victim.quote: She was the daughter of Francesco Cenci

closed_world.value: The Cenci family circle and their associates in Rome and the castle
closed_world.quote: they were at first confined in the Cenci palace under a strong guard

culprit_and_motive.value: Beatrice, Lucrezia, Giacomo and Bernardo Cenci, who hired assassins to kill Francesco
culprit_and_motive.quote: Warrants were immediately issued for the arrest of Giacomo, Bernardo, Lucrezia, and Beatrice

resolution.value: Torture-extracted confession from Marzio and bloodstained sheet evidence led to arrests and executions
resolution.quote: He was put to the torture, and confessed everything. His deposition was sent to Rome

investigator.value: Roman court authorities and Monsignor Guerra conducting the investigation
investigator.quote: The evidence was sent to the Roman court

suspect_architecture.value: Four family conspirators: Giacomo (eldest brother), Bernardo (youngest brother), Lucrezia (stepmother), and Beatrice (daughter) - all with shared motive of escaping Francesco's tyranny, using hired assassins Marzio and Olympio as instruments
suspect_architecture.quote: Warrants were immediately issued for the arrest of Giacomo, Bernardo, Lucrezia, and Beatrice

red_herring.value: The initial focus on external bandits and robbers around the castle, when the true conspiracy was internal family members using hired assassins
red_herring.quote: No incriminating proofs, however, were found, except in the evidence of the laundress

clue_fairness.value: The bloodstained sheet given to the laundress - a physical clue available to both investigators and readers that contradicts the family's innocent explanations
clue_fairness.quote: Beatrice had given her a bloodstained sheet to wash

social_world.value: Roman Catholic Church hierarchy and papal authority structure, with Monsignor Guerra as intermediary wielding ecclesiastical power to orchestrate cover-ups and eliminate witnesses
social_world.quote: Monsignor Guerra received private information that, shortly before the death of Francesco

alibi.value: The family's obstinate denial of complicity and claimed ignorance of the assassins despite evidence linking them to the hired killers
alibi.quote: they obstinately denied both any complicity in the crime and any knowledge of the assassin

reveal_mechanic.value: Torture-extracted confession from captured assassin Marzio, followed by direct confrontation between the confessing killer and the accused family members
reveal_mechanic.quote: He was put to the torture, and confessed everything

media_and_audience.value: The tragic story transformed into artistic memorial - Beatrice's portrait in the Barberini Gallery and the narrative itself as historical chronicle preserving the 'sanguinary history'
media_and_audience.quote: pay a visit to the Barberini Gallery, where you will see, with five other masterpieces by Guido, the portrait of Beatrice
